If you sent more than one message, it will try to route all of them to `forkId`."},"switchChainAndRouteMessage(uint256[])":{"notice":"To be called after the sending of the cross-chain message (`ccipSend`).          Override variant of the `switchChainAndRouteMessage(uint256 forkId)` function in case of multiple destination forks.          Goes through the list of past logs and looks for the `CCIPSendRequested` and `CCIPMessageSent` events.          Loops through provided `forkIds` and tries to route the message to correct destination.          If you haven't provide correct `forkId`, the message will get lost.          If you sent more than one message, it will try to route all of them to correct destinations."}},"notice":"Works with Foundry params.chainId: - The blockchain network chain ID. For example 11155111 for Ethereum Sepolia. Not CCIP chain selector.

returns._0: networkDetails - The tuple containing:          chainSelector - The unique CCIP Chain Selector.          routerAddress - The address of the CCIP Router contract.          linkAddress - The address of the LINK token.          wrappedNativeAddress - The address of the wrapped native token that can be used for CCIP fees.          ccipBnMAddress - The address of the CCIP BnM token.          ccipLnMAddress - The address of the CCIP LnM token.

params.amount: - The amount of LINK tokens to send.
params.to: - The address to which LINK tokens are to be sent.

returns.success: - Returns `true` if the transfer of tokens was successful, otherwise `false`.

params.chainId: - The blockchain network chain ID. For example 11155111 for Ethereum Sepolia. Not CCIP chain selector.
params.networkDetails: - The tuple containing:          chainSelector - The unique CCIP Chain Selector.          routerAddress - The address of the CCIP Router contract.          linkAddress - The address of the LINK token.          wrappedNativeAddress - The address of the wrapped native token that can be used for CCIP fees.          ccipBnMAddress - The address of the CCIP BnM token.          ccipLnMAddress - The address of the CCIP LnM token.

params.forkId: - The ID of the destination network fork. This is the returned value of `createFork()` or `createSelectFork()`

params.forkIds: - The IDs of the destination network forks. These are the returned values of `createFork()` or `createSelectFork()`

targetSenders().details: Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist).

getNetworkDetails(uint256).notice: Returns the default values for currently CCIP supported networks. If network is not present or some of the values are changed, user can manually add new network details using the `setNetworkDetails` function.

requestLinkFromFaucet(address,uint256).notice: Requests LINK tokens from the faucet. The provided amount of tokens are transferred to provided destination address.

setNetworkDetails(uint256,(uint64,addres.notice: If network details are not present or some of the values are changed, user can manually add new network details using the `setNetworkDetails` function.

switchChainAndRouteMessage(uint256).notice: To be called after the sending of the cross-chain message (`ccipSend`).          Goes through the list of past logs and looks for the `CCIPSendRequested` and `CCIPMessageSent` events.          Switches to a destination network fork. Routes the sent cross-chain message on the destination network.          If you sent more than one message, it will try to route all of them to `forkId`.

switchChainAndRouteMessage(uint256[]).notice: To be called after the sending of the cross-chain message (`ccipSend`).          Override variant of the `switchChainAndRouteMessage(uint256 forkId)` function in case of multiple destination forks.          Goes through the list of past logs and looks for the `CCIPSendRequested` and `CCIPMessageSent` events.          Loops through provided `forkIds` and tries to route the message to correct destination.          If you haven't provide correct `forkId`, the message will get lost.          If you sent more than one message, it will try to route all of them to correct destinations.
